pattern: Every letter only ever has two routes to its target -- walk the alphabet forward or walk it backward -- so the total answer is just the cheaper of those two routes summed independently per position.
hints: Each character's transformation is independent of every other character's -- solve position by position and add the results.

For one character, simulate walking forward (charging nextCost at each letter you leave) all the way to the target, and separately walking backward (charging previousCost at each letter you leave); the letters wrap from z to a and back.
Take whichever of the two walking directions is cheaper for that character, and sum that minimum across every position in the string.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, uniform cost of 1 everywhere
single character needing a full wraparound either way
zero cost in both directions everywhere
identical strings, no shifting needed at all

stress.description: random lowercase strings with random cost tables
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 20000))
    import string
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(n))
    t = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(n))
    next_cost = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(26)]
    prev_cost = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(26)]
    return [s, t, next_cost, prev_cost]
